message:
test(readme): cover all 9 .cat() methods in categorical test (fd90.195 / br-o6qo4)

Extends readme_categorical_analysis_compiles_and_runs to cover the 5
remaining .cat() accessor methods documented in README line 422:

- add_categories (3 → 4 categories)
- remove_unused_categories (back to 3 after dropping unobserved)
- set_categories (replace category set entirely)
- as_ordered / as_unordered (toggle ordered flag, verified via the
  CategoricalAccessor.ordered() reader)

The original test already covered categories(), codes(), rename_categories(),
and to_values(). With this extension, all 9 methods listed in the
README are now exercised.

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — the categorical test
covered 4 of the 9 methods enumerated in the README.

Verified: 33 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
